behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is a backoff-policy calculation, i.e., local retry-delay logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required to expose a wrong doubling or cap formula. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected Integration (L+1): behavior text is purely policy math — "wait time ... doubles ... up to a configured maximum" — with no required module boundary or shared state handoff in the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Test the delay-calculation function/object directly with consecutive failure counts and max-cap config; assert exponential sequence and cap saturation (e.g., 100, 200, 400, 800, 1000, 1000).

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is response-classification + retry policy handoff across modules (response parser/transport wrapper -> retry engine). REAL-INFRA: no real network needed; crafted in-process responses reproduce the failure. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only the calculator could miss misclassification wiring. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): "identifies a rate-limit response" plus "computes ... wait time before the next attempt" implies cross-module handoff, not just one pure function. Rejected System (L+1): behavior does not require real HTTP timing/socket behavior; deterministic in-process fake responses are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry module with a fake transport that returns realistic rate-limit and non-rate-limit response objects/headers; assert only rate-limit responses trigger retry path and computed wait uses configured policy/headers.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration across timer/backoff + request execution + response return path. REAL-INFRA: fake clock and fake transport can reproduce resend and transparency failures without real network/OS timing. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): "after the wait, the client actually resends the original request" requires interaction between waiting and transport modules. Rejected System (L+1): no requirement for real HTTP stack; behavior is about resend semantics, not network realism.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that fails with rate-limit once then succeeds; assert same request payload is resent after scheduled delay and caller receives final success value with no retry internals leaked.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is retry-counter exhaustion + error mapping/type propagation across modules. REAL-INFRA: in-process failing transport is sufficient; real network not needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): "after the configured maximum number of retries" requires loop state plus terminal mapping, not just isolated constructor logic. Rejected System (L+1): no OS/subprocess/real HTTP behavior is needed to validate typed terminal error selection.
behaviors.test_description: With fake transport always returning rate-limit, run through retry orchestrator to exhaustion and assert thrown/rejected error is `QuotaExhaustedError` (type and fields), not generic upstream error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is end-to-end local orchestration of retry state machine and quota tracker. REAL-INFRA: fake clock + in-process stubs catch non-termination; no real OS/network primitive is required. MOCK-HIDE: overly isolated unit tests can miss loop conditions caused by module interaction. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI steps. Rejected Unit (L-1): "full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking" explicitly spans multiple modules/states. Rejected System (L+1): infinite-loop risk here is logic-level in orchestration, reproducible without real HTTP/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run integrated retry pipeline with deterministic failing responses and fake timers; assert completion within bounded virtual time/attempt count and terminal error is surfaced exactly once (no unbounded scheduling).

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at shared-state/concurrency boundary across caller-facing client instances/tasks. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process async concurrency; no real network needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): "concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process" and "same shared quota bucket" requires multi-module/shared-state interaction. Rejected System (L+1): process-local concurrency semantics are testable without real external API calls.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple concurrent `call()` invocations against shared client/quota manager with stub transport; assert aggregate token/attempt accounting is global across callers and quota exhaustion is enforced consistently.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs emerge in queue + scheduler + dispatcher interaction, not single-function logic alone. REAL-INFRA: deterministic in-process queue with fake timers/concurrency is sufficient; real network/OS does not add required failure signal. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): "multiple requests are waiting" and "dispatched in the order ... submitted" is cross-component scheduling behavior. Rejected System (L+1): FIFO property can be validated without real HTTP or subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue several blocked requests via concurrent callers, then release capacity progressively; assert dispatch/transport invocation order matches submission order exactly (FIFO) under contention.
